topic: DryRun Security Review: AI SAST Built for Agentic Coding Workflows

key_points: Started as SCA tool, added SAST later — hybrid approach
Catches only ~38% of planted vulnerabilities in head-to-head tests
Misses all logic-level flaws (IDOR, broken auth, user enumeration)
Stronger SCA platform; Forrester Wave Leader Q4 2024
Supports ~17 languages for SAST
Common enterprise pattern: Snyk for SCA + Semgrep for SAST
Pattern-matching SAST engine expanded into SCA
Covers 35+ languages across GA, beta, and experimental tiers
Finds less than half of known vulnerabilities in benchmark tests
Outperforms Snyk on pure SAST (EASE 2024 benchmark)
Requires teams to curate and maintain rule files — higher operational overhead
DryRun comparison: 'Semgrep matches patterns; DryRun understands behavior'
GitHub Advanced Security (CodeQL) — native GitHub integration
Deep integration for GitHub-hosted repos
Strong for C/C++, Java, JavaScript, Python, Go
Slower analysis; better suited for comprehensive batch scans than PR-speed feedback
Listed among top 10 AI SAST tools for 2026 by DryRun Security
Enterprise-focused; Checkmarx One is unified cloud-native AppSec platform
Combines SAST, SCA, IaC, API, DAST, container, and supply chain security
Includes ASPM and agentic AI agents (Developer Assist)
Higher cost and complexity; best for large security teams
Listed among top 12 AI developer tools for 2026
Popular open-source SAST with SonarCloud managed service
Strong for code quality + security combined
Large community; broad language support
DryRun comparison showed DryRun outperforming in contextual/logic flaw detection
Listed among top 10 AI SAST tools for 2026

stats.fact: DryRun Security's CSA engine detected 88% of all seeded vulnerabilities including logic-level flaws; nearest competitor caught less than half
stats.source: DryRun Security SAST Accuracy Report — dryrun.security/sast-accuracy-report

stats.fact: Snyk Code surfaced only ~38% of planted vulnerabilities; missed every logic-level flaw (IDOR, broken auth, user enumeration)
stats.source: DryRun Security vs. Snyk, CodeQL, SonarQube, and Semgrep Python/Django Showdown — dryrun.security/blog

angles: Why AI-generated code demands a new class of SAST: pattern-matching tools miss 50%+ of agentic code flaws
